objective: Bootstrap Agent Integrity OS v0.1 as an English-first OSS starter repository for AI-generated work integrity checks.
what_changed: Created Protocol / Schema / Core / CLI / GitHub Action / Benchmark / Docs repository structure
Implemented deterministic PASS / DELAY / BLOCK scoring engine without external API or LLM calls
Added CLI command for checking completion-report.json files
Added composite GitHub Action wrapper with GitHub Step Summary output
Added 30 benchmark cases with expected PASS / DELAY / BLOCK outcomes
Added README, specification, scoring model, handoff standard, whitepaper, roadmap, security, citation, and contribution files

evidence_anchor.label: Benchmark suite
evidence_anchor.value: npm run benchmark returned 30 passed, 0 failed

evidence_anchor.label: CLI examples
evidence_anchor.value: PASS / DELAY / BLOCK examples returned expected statuses

unresolved: v0.1 uses JavaScript ESM for zero-dependency execution; TypeScript declarations/build pipeline can be added in v0.1.1 if desired
GitHub Marketplace publication still requires pushing this repo to GitHub and tagging a release
MCP server is planned for v0.2
OpenSSF/SLSA hardening is planned for v0.3
must_preserve: Core scoring remains deterministic and inspectable
No external API calls, telemetry, or LLM-based judgment in v0.1
Exit code contract: PASS=0, DELAY=1, BLOCK=2
Completion is not integrity remains the core public message
rollback_path: Delete or revert the generated agent-integrity-os directory before publishing, or revert the initial Git commit after repository creation.
restart_point: Create a new GitHub repository named agent-integrity-os, copy this directory, run npm test && npm run build && npm run benchmark, then commit with: feat: bootstrap Agent Integrity OS v0.1
stop_condition: Stop before publishing if npm test, npm run build, or npm run benchmark fails, or if any secrets are added to the repository.
